questions.question: Derive the formula for the area of a sector of a circle.
questions.answer: Using the unitary method: area of full circle = πr² for 360°, so area for θ° = (θ/360) × πr².

questions.question: Derive the formula for the length of an arc of a sector.
questions.answer: Circumference of circle = 2πr for 360°, so arc length for θ° = (θ/360) × 2πr.

questions.question: Explain how to find the area of a segment of a circle.
questions.answer: First find the area of the sector, then subtract the area of the triangle formed by the radii and chord.

questions.question: Why is the triangle formed in a segment an isosceles triangle?
questions.answer: Because two sides of the triangle are radii of the same circle and hence equal.

questions.question: Find the area of a sector of radius r and angle 180°.
What is the area of the major sector if the minor sector angle is 60°?
questions.answer: Major sector angle = 360° − 60° = 300°, so area = (300/360) × πr².

questions.question: Explain why the area of a major segment is always greater than a minor segment.
questions.answer: Because the major segment covers more than half of the circle.

questions.question: Find the area of a quadrant of radius 14 cm.
questions.answer: Area = (1/4) × π × 14² = 49π cm².

questions.question: What happens to the arc length when the radius is doubled?
questions.answer: The arc length also doubles.

questions.question: Explain the role of trigonometry in finding the area of a segment.
questions.answer: Trigonometric ratios are used to find the height of the triangle formed inside the segment.

questions.question: Find the area of a circle whose circumference is 44 cm.
questions.answer: 2πr = 44 ⇒ r = 7 cm, so area = π × 7² = 49π cm².

questions.question: Why is the angle at the centre used in sector formulas?
questions.answer: Because the size of a sector depends on the central angle it subtends.

questions.question: Find the area of a sector if radius is 7 cm and angle is 90°.
Explain why the area of a full sector equals the area of the circle.
questions.answer: Because a full sector subtends an angle of 360° at the centre.

questions.question: What is the area of a segment if the area of sector is equal to the area of triangle?
questions.answer: The area of the segment is zero.

questions.question: Find the arc length of a semicircle of radius r.
questions.answer: Arc length = (180/360) × 2πr = πr.

questions.question: What is the sum of angles around a point?
questions.answer: The sum of angles around a point is 360 degrees.

questions.question: Find the area of a sector if radius is r and angle is θ radians.
questions.answer: Area = (1/2)r²θ.

questions.question: Explain why the area of a circle depends on the square of its radius.
questions.answer: Because the formula for area is πr², showing area increases with r².

questions.question: What is the area of a circle if r = 1 unit?
questions.answer: The area is π square units.

questions.question: Why is π taken as 22/7 or 3.14 in calculations?
questions.answer: Because π is irrational and its exact value cannot be written.

questions.question: Explain the difference between a sector and a segment.
questions.answer: A sector is bounded by two radii and an arc, while a segment is bounded by a chord and an arc.

questions.question: Find the area of a quadrant if the diameter is 28 cm.
questions.answer: Radius = 14 cm, area = (1/4) × π × 14² = 49π cm².

questions.question: Explain why the radius is perpendicular to the tangent at the point of contact.
questions.answer: The shortest distance from the centre to the tangent is perpendicular.

questions.question: Summarise the steps to find the area of a segment of a circle.
questions.answer: Find area of sector, find area of triangle, then subtract triangle area from sector area.
